affected_groups: State school district centres (uniform enforcement burden)
Employers and higher-education programmes relying on the ability signal
Non-quota-eligible disadvantaged applicants at risk of displacement
content.decision_question: Equity (genuine opportunity for disadvantaged pupils) versus institutional autonomy and administrative/workload feasibility, with academic-ability signalling as a third value in tension.
content.evidence_boundary: Contested/unknown: the independent research voice explicitly flags the evidence status as weak on whether any specific proxy, quota, or exam-redesign mechanism reduces coaching-sensitivity or displacement in practice; no pre-registered pilot evidence yet exists, so both the mechanism's effectiveness and its side effects remain empirical open questions rather than settled facts.
content.tension: SES-aware admission quotas and coaching-resistant exam redesign can reduce entry-gate gatekeeping, but only if exam validity, uniform cross-maintainer enforcement, and non-displacement safeguards for disadvantaged pupils are guaranteed.
content.title: Decision tension A1: S1
value_poles: institutional autonomy and administrative/workload feasibility, with academic-ability signalling as a third value in tension.
